pull.title: coins: delegate `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin` to `GetCoin`
pull.body:
Split out of https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34132#discussion_r2692307054.

**Problem:** `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` currently answers a different question from the cache-backed coins view path.
It calls `CDBWrapper::Exists()`, which only checks whether LevelDB has a value for the coin key, while `GetCoin()` tries to read the value as an unspent `Coin`.
This makes the direct DB view's `HaveCoin()` semantics diverge from `CCoinsViewCache::HaveCoin()`, which fetches through `GetCoin()` on cache misses.
The separate `CDBWrapper::ExistsImpl()` read path also duplicates `ReadImpl()` even though the remaining `CDBWrapper::Exists()` callers are infrequent metadata checks.

**Fix:** Make `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` delegate to `GetCoin()`, so it reports whether the outpoint can be read as an unspent `Coin` (not just whether the database contains any value).
Also have `CDBWrapper::Read()` and `Exists()` share a `ReadRaw()` helper instead of keeping separate LevelDB read implementations.

**Testing:** Add a unit test documenting that `CCoinsViewCache::HaveInputs()` checks the cache first and calls the backing view's `GetCoin()`, not `HaveCoin()`, on cache miss.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/34320. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Concept ACK | [sedited](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320#issuecomment-4011164247) | | Stale ACK | [andrewtoth](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320#pullrequestreview-3802394912), [Eunovo](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320#pullrequestreview-3846623578) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#35465](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35465) (coins: compact chainstate regularly by l0rinc) * [#34931](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34931) (validation: abort on DB unreadable coins instead of treating them as missing by furszy) * [#34132](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34132) (coins: drop error catcher, centralize fatal read handling by l0rinc) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first.

> One tries to deserialize, the other just returns whether any value is associated with the key.
> But we usually need the full value we probed for, and `HaveCoin` cannot cache the returned value, so it can trigger an extra lookup if `HaveCoin` uses `Exists` and the caller later needs the actual value.

Are there any flamegraphs or benchmarks, to see how this affects performance?

> Are there any flamegraphs or benchmarks, to see how this affects performance?

I'm not sure what to measure exactly because the current code was already delegating to `GetCoin` in most places.
The pruned IBD and `-reindex-chainstate` and `AssumeUTXO` loading benchmarks I started haven't finished yet - but I don't expect *any* change there.
If you can describe a scenario that you would find helpful, please let me know.

> Are there any flamegraphs or benchmarks, to see how this affects performance?

Added assumeutxo load, reindex-chainstate and pruned IBD measurements to the PR description

> we usually need the full value we probed for, and HaveCoin cannot cache the returned value, so it can trigger an extra lookup if HaveCoin uses Exists and the caller later needs the actual value.

I see 4 uses of `HaveCoin`
1. In `ConnectBlock`, we ensure we do not already have outputs of a block for BIP30 checks.
2. In `ApplyTxInUndo`, we ensure we do not already have the coin we are about to roll back to unspent.
3. In `MemPoolAccept::PreChecks`, where we check that we have all inputs of a tx we are accepting to the mempool.
4. In `TxVerify`, where we call `HaveInputs`.

For 1 and 2, we are checking for the non-existence of the coin so we won't need the full value. In this case not caching the value makes sense.
For 3, it is explicitly relying on the fact that the value is cached. The backend is thrown away after all input prevouts are added to the top level cache.
For 4, it is a peformance improvement that the value is cached.

I think 1 and 2 would benefit from `CCoinsViewCache::HaveCoin` delegating to the base's `HaveCoin` and not going through `FetchCoin`.
For 3 this seems like an abuse of the `CCoinsView` API. But, calling `GetCoin` instead there would result in unnecessary copying of the `Coin`. I'm not sure what the best solution is here.
For 4 the current behavior makes sense.

> only delegates to GetCoin if there's a cache miss. In CCoinsViewCache::HaveCoin we avoid copying Coin

Disassembly with separate translation units indicates `GetCoin()` does a new + memcpy of the Coin and the caller immediately deletes it, even when only checking `.has_value()`. `HaveCoin()` does indeed avoid it, but the extra copy only happens on the unexpectedly-present coin path, since if it's expected, we usually want to cache it. If we want to avoid the extra copy, we can use `AccessCoin`, but we would duplicate the spentness check.

> In this case not caching the value makes sense.

But there's nothing to cache since these will return an empty optional. For BIP30 and `ApplyTxInUndo` the missing-coin path dominates, so `GetCoin()` short-circuits on `Read()` failure without deserialization, so I/O is the same as `Exists()`, no coin-copy happens (and when it does we crash or reorg anyway). But given that validation until bip30-end takes less than 3 minutes on my benchmarking server (see https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33817), I'd say it's irrelevant either way.

> For 3 this seems like an abuse of the CCoinsView API. But, calling GetCoin instead there would result in unnecessary copying of the Coin. I'm not sure what the best solution is here.

This is a good point, the disassembly indicates there's no devirtualization and no inlining in `CCoinsViewCache::HaveInputs` and `MemPoolAccept::PreChecks`, so the copy is real - though a lot faster than disk access, so it's not actually measurable.
We can use `AccessCoin` here and with explicit `IsSpent` call instead which would avoid the call (according to the assembly):
```C++
if (AccessCoin(tx.vin[i].prevout).IsSpent()) {
```
and
```C++
if (m_view.AccessCoin(txin.prevout).IsSpent()) {
```

----

I have added a new benchmark as a first commit to check the performance of on-disk `HaveInputs` to make sure the performance is retained.

Benchmark baseline:
|               ns/tx |                tx/s |    err% |     total | benchmark
|--------------------:|--------------------:|--------:|----------:|:----------
|              998.95 |        1,001,046.69 |    0.8% |      1.09 | `HaveInputsOnDisk`

Benchmark with `!GetCoin()` instead of `HaveCoin`:

|               ns/tx |                tx/s |    err% |     total | benchmark
|--------------------:|--------------------:|--------:|----------:|:----------
|              985.57 |        1,014,640.63 |    0.8% |      1.10 | `HaveInputsOnDisk`

Replace remaining `HaveCoin` calls with `GetCoin()`/`AccessCoin` and remove the former

|               ns/tx |                tx/s |    err% |     total | benchmark
|--------------------:|--------------------:|--------:|----------:|:----------
|              983.17 |        1,017,118.55 |    0.3% |      1.10 | `HaveInputsOnDisk`

(ran it 5 times for each commit and copied the fastest (with smallest noise). The small difference is reproducible, but not very important as long as it's not measurably slower)

<details>

```bash

done
```

> If we want to avoid the extra copy, we can use AccessCoin, but we would duplicate the spentness check.

This tells us that `HaveCoin` is the right level of abstraction then, and is a useful method, no?

> In this case not caching the value makes sense.
>>But there's nothing to cache since these will return an empty optional

Same as above.

> We can use AccessCoin here and with explicit IsSpent call instead which would avoid the call (according to the assembly)

I think everywhere we use AccessCoin we assert the unspentness. This would now break that pattern and use AccessCoin to check existence via spentness. I think HaveCoin conveys intent better here.

Can we instead just remove HaveCoin on the db then, and the default implementation would just forward to `!!GetCoin` for it?

Thanks @andrewtoth, I didn't like the suggestion at first since we keep polluting the interface, but after applying your suggestion I'm fine with the results, it's simpler and better separated from the other similar PRs.

ACK d1a8a1bc5692e1b38932f3bef36da8500614e569

`CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin` is dead code, good to remove it and prevent confusion.
Also, adding test coverage and a benchmark to prevent regressions is good.

Concept ACK https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320/commits/ff338fdb53a66ab40a36e1277e7371941fc89840:

Left some comments.
Looks good, but I have one more comment on the test.
ACK https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320/commits/8bfd863f4c863260db404dbf0b9e4d172c6e5a72

Concept ACK

I'm a bit confused by this patch set. If the `CCoinsViewDB`'s `HaveCoin` is only used in the coins tests to verify invariants (which can be checked by replacing its implementation with a `throw`), why is this adding a benchmark? I'm also not sure why this is also changing the implementation of `Exists` if it is not used by production code in the coins db path. Can't that just be left alone? Why not scope this PR to just remove the `HaveCoin` member function and add the unit test from the second commit?

> why is this adding a benchmark? 

I touched on that briefly in the commit message:
> `CCoinsViewCache::HaveInputs()` is used by `Consensus::CheckTxInputs()` and currently exercises the `HaveCoin()` path in the UTXO cache.
Performance could be affected due to `Coin` copying and different cache-warming behavior.

The motivation was @andrewtoth's concern about a coin copy regression. See https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320#issuecomment-3795574265

It also checks the cached behavior, not just the DB query.

> I'm also not sure why this is also changing the implementation of Exists 

I tried to cover this in the commit message as well. Please let me know which part needs more detail:
>`ExistsImpl` was removed since it duplicates `CDBWrapper::ReadImpl`.

Basically, we can remove it now since the underlying premise, trying to avoid deserialization cost, isn't warranted anymore. I could inline the `Exists` method now (I did so in a previous version), but this results in a smaller diff.

> The motivation was @andrewtoth's concern about a coin copy regression. See https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320#issuecomment-3795574265

Why do these concerns apply to its removal if the coins db's `HaveCoin` is never called in production?

> Basically, we can remove it now since the underlying premise, trying to avoid deserialization cost, isn't warranted anymore. I could inline the Exists method now (I did so in a previous version), but this results in a smaller diff.

Sure, but it would be good to mention why this cost isn't relevant to its current use in the indexes.

Inconclusive review c68262b6cf068d55faf555f4dcc51a92d53a4fdb

These questions around `HaveCoin` arise due to caching: without caching, it's clear that it makes sense to have a way to check for existence without returning the value. With caching in the mix the question arises whether in a certain use case it makes sense to bring the value into the cache or not, and the answer is context-specific.

If there was a cache-instance-level control of the cache view read-write level -- read-only, or read-only-but-cache-mutable, or write -- then the cache instance could cache or not accordingly. In the current implementation, this is not the case, so behavior can be controlled at the call level.

Several options are possible:
1. `HaveCoin` also caches (whenever a cache is available); non-caching existence check is not possible (as it doesn't make much sense)
2. `HaveCoin` doesn't cache; caching existence check is possible with `!!GetCoin()`.
3. A caching and non-caching version of `HaveCoin` (this is probably overkill).

This PR implements option 1. Following the discussions here (and in #35078), this makes the most sense.

My review is inconclusive as of now, because:

- This PR includes #35078, which is still being discussed
- I see some inconsistencies in the current state
- In the current version, both `HaveCoin` and `GetCoin` are used, and `GetCoin` behavior is forked into returning and peek-only versions. I think the current `HaveCoin` could be used for the (caching) peek-only version, and then there is no need for changing `GetCoin`.
- The PR title seems outdated: `HaveCoin` is not removed, only `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin` behavior is changed.

The test additions and cleanups are very valuable, but I feel there are some inconsistencies in the current form that should be addressed.
Rebased, removed the contentious [#35078](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35078) squash - ready for review again.

test: add `HaveInputs` call-path unit tests

Add unit tests covering `CCoinsViewCache::HaveInputs()`.
The tests document that `HaveInputs()` consults the cache first and that a cache miss pulls from the backing view via `GetCoin()`.
The spy view inherits from `CoinsViewEmpty` so only the `GetCoin()`/`HaveCoin()` interaction under test is customized.

Co-authored-by: Novo <eunovo9@gmail.com>

verification.payload:
tree 97e3389cb09be1df7d49e330f643fa30907ea81d
parent 3b3ebc65edfc71f775538a6088817f117271db9a
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1770630210 +0100
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1779434683 +0300

refactor: split out `CDBWrapper::ReadRaw` from `CDBWrapper::Read`

Split the raw LevelDB lookup into `CDBWrapper::ReadRaw()` so later commits can change typed `Read()` deserialization behavior without duplicating key serialization.

Move `Write()` below the read and existence helpers, and return from successful iterator decode paths inside the `try` blocks so this commit stays behavior-neutral.

events.message:
refactor: split out `CDBWrapper::ReadRaw` from `CDBWrapper::Read`

Split the raw LevelDB lookup into `CDBWrapper::ReadRaw()` so later commits can change typed `Read()` deserialization behavior without duplicating key serialization.

Move `Write()` below the read and existence helpers, and return from successful iterator decode paths inside the `try` blocks so this commit stays behavior-neutral.

verification.payload:
tree 2fb445b272fc291e874fc66aa1d7908eacb5539f
parent 44c276d8f7666e12ab92b1b4590ab0a2b9f3a355
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1768430928 +0100
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1779434683 +0300

dbwrapper: have `Read` and `Exists` reuse `ReadRaw`

`ExistsImpl` duplicates `CDBWrapper::ReadImpl()`: both issue a LevelDB `Get`, but only `ReadImpl()` keeps the returned bytes.

Introduce a small helper returning the raw value bytes as `std::string`, make `Read()` deserialize from it, and make `Exists()` just check whether the raw read succeeded.
After this series, `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` also goes through `GetCoin()` / `Read()`, while the remaining `CDBWrapper::Exists()` callers are infrequent metadata checks, so reusing `ReadRaw()` does not affect a performance-sensitive path.

Introduce a small helper returning the raw value bytes as `std::string`, make `Read()` deserialize from it, and make `Exists()` just check whether the raw read succeeded.
After this series, `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` also goes through `GetCoin()` / `Read()`, while the remaining `CDBWrapper::Exists()` callers are infrequent metadata checks, so reusing `ReadRaw()` does not affect a performance-sensitive path.

verification.payload:
tree d393a54aca00dcc818ee77d9bbf5d6013d10a42d
parent adaecfbe4c026175b108ca6af7055a3d46970559
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1769950968 +0100
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1779434683 +0300

coins: delegate `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin` to `GetCoin`

`m_db->Exists(CoinEntry(&outpoint))` only checks whether some value is stored for the coin key, while `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` should match `GetCoin()` and report whether the outpoint can be read as an unspent `Coin`.

`CCoinsViewCache::HaveCoin()` already goes through `FetchCoin()` and `GetCoin()` on cache misses, so keep the required override but make it a thin adapter to `GetCoin()` instead of giving `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` its own `Exists()`-based semantics.

events.message:
coins: delegate `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin` to `GetCoin`

`m_db->Exists(CoinEntry(&outpoint))` only checks whether some value is stored for the coin key, while `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` should match `GetCoin()` and report whether the outpoint can be read as an unspent `Coin`.

`CCoinsViewCache::HaveCoin()` already goes through `FetchCoin()` and `GetCoin()` on cache misses, so keep the required override but make it a thin adapter to `GetCoin()` instead of giving `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` its own `Exists()`-based semantics.

events.body:
> As I see, what this PR solves is the code duplication across ReadImpl and ExistsImpl

That's part of the reason. The other reason here is that `HaveCoin()` is a coins-view API, not a raw DB-key API. A value that exists in LevelDB but cannot be decoded as an unspent `Coin` should not satisfy that contract. This also matches the direction of #34132/#34931, where unreadable coin entries should go through the typed read path instead of being treated as successful existence checks.

> I think a simpler solution is to get rid of ExistsImpl, and replace it with !!ReadImpl. Unless I miss something, this achieves the same.

That wouldn't attempt deserialization and wouldn't cache the results.

I have rebased and dropped the benchmark for simplicity.

> A value that exists in LevelDB but cannot be decoded as an unspent `Coin` should not satisfy that contract.

That depends how the contract is understood/defined.
Can you explain a non-exceptional case when this can happen? (Outside edge cases like a corrupt database, that eventually results in a critical failure, so it's mostly irrelevant.)

> That wouldn't attempt deserialization and wouldn't cache the results.

Can you elaborate more on caching? I can't see additional caching introduced with this change:

- `CDBWrapper::Exists()' is affected, but ultimately the same operations are invoked (post-change), I see no change in caching.
- `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` is affected, but ultimately the same operations are invoked (post-change), with the exception of extra `Assert(!coin.IsSpent())` check, no change in caching.
- `CCoinsViewCache::HaveCoin()` is *not* affected, and it does perform caching (even pre-change), as it calls `FetchCoin()`.

This last point also means that for all usage through `CCoinsViewCache` -- which is virtually all UTXO set handling -- `HaveCoin` does go through deobfuscation and deserialization anyways.

<summary>
Detailed pre- and post- call chain analysis follows (LLM-aided)
<details>

## `CDBWrapper::Exists()` -- Call Chain Comparison

### Current Version (with local changes)

```
CDBWrapper::Exists(key)                        [dbwrapper.h:238-241]
  return !!ReadRaw(key)
    │
    └─► CDBWrapper::ReadRaw(key)               [dbwrapper.h:213-219]
          ssKey << key  // serialize
          return ReadImpl(ssKey)
            │
            └─► CDBWrapper::ReadImpl(span<byte>) [dbwrapper.cpp:315-327]
                  leveldb::DB::Get(readoptions, slKey, &strValue)
                  if IsNotFound → return nullopt
                  if other error → LogError + HandleError (throws)
                  return strValue              // full value returned as optional<string>
    │
    // optional<string> propagates back up through ReadRaw → Exists
    // Exists discards the value, returns just !!optional
```

### Original Version (without local changes)

```
CDBWrapper::Exists(key)                        [old dbwrapper.h]
  ssKey << key  // serialize
  return ExistsImpl(ssKey)
    │
    └─► CDBWrapper::ExistsImpl(span<byte>)     [old dbwrapper.cpp, now deleted]
          leveldb::DB::Get(readoptions, slKey, &strValue)
          if IsNotFound → return false
          if other error → LogError + HandleError (throws)
          return true                          // bool only; strValue dies here
```

---

### Key Differences

| Aspect | Original | Current |
|---|---|---|
| Private impl method | `ExistsImpl` (dedicated) | `ReadImpl` (shared with `Read`) |
| LevelDB call | `pdb->Get()` | `pdb->Get()` (same) |
| Value after LevelDB `Get` | `strValue` is local, destroyed immediately | `strValue` returned as `optional<string>`, propagated up two levels, then discarded by `!!` |
| String lifetime | Never leaves `ExistsImpl` | Escapes `ReadImpl`, escapes `ReadRaw`, discarded in `Exists` |
| Code reuse | `Exists` and `Read` had separate impl paths | `Exists` reuses `ReadRaw` → `ReadImpl` |
| Stack overhead | Lower — no string move up stack | Extra `std::string` move through two return values |

---

### Notes

- Both versions call `leveldb::DB::Get()`, which always fetches the **full value**. LevelDB has no cheaper "key probe" API, so there is **no I/O difference**.
- The only runtime cost difference is the `std::string` move through two return levels in the current version, which the compiler may optimize away.
- The trade-off is **fewer lines of code** (reuse of `ReadRaw`) in exchange for that minor overhead.

---

## `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin()` — Call Chain Comparison

### Current Version (with local changes)

```
CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin(outpoint)               [txdb.cpp:87-90]
  return !!GetCoin(outpoint)
    │
    └─► CCoinsViewDB::GetCoin(outpoint)         [txdb.cpp:73-80]
          m_db->Read(CoinEntry(&outpoint), coin)
            │
            └─► CDBWrapper::Read(key, value)    [dbwrapper.h:222-235]
                  ReadRaw(key)
                    │
                    └─► CDBWrapper::ReadRaw(key) [dbwrapper.h:213-219]
                          ssKey << key  // serialize CoinEntry
                          return ReadImpl(ssKey)
                            │
                            └─► CDBWrapper::ReadImpl(span<byte>) [dbwrapper.cpp:315-327]
                                  leveldb::DB::Get(readoptions, slKey, &strValue)
                                  if IsNotFound → return nullopt
                                  if other error → LogError + HandleError (throws)
                                  return strValue  // optional<string>
                  if (!strValue) return false
                  m_obfuscation(ssValue)           // XOR deobfuscation
                  SpanReader{ssValue} >> coin      // deserialize into Coin object
                  return true
          if Read returned false → return nullopt
          Assert(!coin.IsSpent())                  // sanity check
          return coin                              // optional<Coin> with value
    │
    // optional<Coin> propagates back to HaveCoin
    // !! discards the fully-deserialized Coin, returns just bool
```

### Original Version (without local changes)

```
CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin(outpoint)               [old txdb.cpp]
  return m_db->Exists(CoinEntry(&outpoint))
    │
    └─► CDBWrapper::Exists(key)                [old dbwrapper.h]
          ssKey << key  // serialize CoinEntry
          return ExistsImpl(ssKey)
            │
            └─► CDBWrapper::ExistsImpl(span<byte>) [old dbwrapper.cpp, now deleted]
                  leveldb::DB::Get(readoptions, slKey, &strValue)
                  if IsNotFound → return false
                  if other error → LogError + HandleError (throws)
                  return true                  // bool only; strValue discarded
```

---

### Key Differences

| Aspect | Original | Current |
|---|---|---|
| Call depth | 3 levels (`HaveCoin` → `Exists` → `ExistsImpl`) | 6 levels (`HaveCoin` → `GetCoin` → `Read` → `ReadRaw` → `ReadImpl` → LevelDB) |
| LevelDB call | `pdb->Get()` | `pdb->Get()` (same) |
| Deobfuscation | None — raw bytes discarded | Yes — XOR applied to the value bytes |
| Deserialization | None — raw bytes discarded | Yes — full `Coin` object constructed |
| Sanity check | None | `Assert(!coin.IsSpent())` |
| Work discarded | Raw `strValue` string (cheap) | Fully constructed `Coin` object (expensive) |
| Return path | `bool` returned directly from `ExistsImpl` | `optional<Coin>` bubbles up, collapsed to `bool` by `!!` |

---

### Notes

- Both versions issue **one** `leveldb::DB::Get()` call — there is no I/O difference.
- The current version does substantially more **CPU work** for the same boolean result: it deobfuscates the raw bytes and deserializes them into a full `Coin` object (including reading `CTxOut`, amount, script, block height, coinbase flag), then immediately throws it all away via `!!`.
- The original `ExistsImpl` discarded `strValue` before it ever left the function, never touching the value bytes beyond the LevelDB read.
- The regression is a direct consequence of removing the dedicated `ExistsImpl` path and rerouting `HaveCoin` through `GetCoin` for code-reuse reasons.
</details>
</summary>

I find that it's relatively painless to remove `HaveCoin` from the `CCoinsView` interface (and from `CCoinsViewDB`), and keep only in the `CCoinsViewCache` class. `HaveCoin` is mostly called only on `CCoinsViewCache` (not on the interface!). (CC @sedited )

If the real goal here is slimming the interface, I suggest to do that and state that (but the current form of this PR I still find incoherent).

For minimal changes to remove `HaveCoin`, see https://github.com/optout21/bitcoin/pull/15 .

Continuing, I think the real design question raised here is: Is `CCoinsView` an interface:

A. For all coin stores, including `CCoinsViewCache`, or
B. For all coin stores that can be *beneath* `CCoinsViewCache` (but excluding itself)

A. does not fully hold currently, as there are several methods of `CCoinsViewCache` that are not on the interface, and invoked directly (`HaveCoinInCache`, `AccessCoin`, `HaveInputs`, etc.).
If `HaveCoin` is removed from the interface, that goes towards B., which is fine, as long as the scope of the interface is clear/clarified.

yes, isn't that using tmpfs?
> Many Unix [distributions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Distribution_(software)) enable and use tmpfs by default for the [/tmp](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki//tmp) branch of the file system or for [shared memory](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shared_memory_(interprocess_communication)).

and
> tmpfs (short for temporary file system) is a [temporary file](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temporary_file) storage paradigm implemented in many [Unix-like](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unix-like) operating systems. It is intended to appear as a mounted [file system](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File_system), but data is stored in [volatile memory](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Volatile_memory) rather than on a persistent storage device.

this looks like it is re-inventing the wheel in a brittle and confusing way. You can use `BasicTestingSetup` to get a m_path_root, which you can join with `bench.name()` to get your temp coinsview db path. The current code seems not ideal for several reasons:

* It is re-inventing the wheel for no given reason, which is confusing
* It is using the deprecated `GetTime` helper
* It is brittle, because it does create a temp dir, bug ignores the `-testdatadir` arg.
* Manual cleanup code is added below, when a context manager could take care of it instead.

comments.body:
The distinction I have is that a test assumes a hypothesis of precise behavior, we assert against expected values. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software_testing writes:
> Software testing can determine the correctness of software for specific scenarios

Strictly speaking, benchmarks are for measuring change, not testing expectations or correctness, it's mostly meant for catching regressions.
I wouldn't call the compiler or linter or static analysis tests (even though they are definitely checking for correctness), but I don't mind if others do, I was simply explaining why:
```C++
const auto path{fs::temp_directory_path() / strprintf("bench-%s-%lld", bench.name(), GetTime())};
```
seemed simpler to me than
```C++
const auto testing_setup{MakeNoLogFileContext<const BasicTestingSetup>(ChainType::REGTEST)};
const auto path{testing_setup->m_path_root / fs::PathFromString(bench.name())};
```

----

But I have already applied your suggestions, thanks for the reviews! :)

comments.body:
> seeed simpler to me than

Right, it seems superficially simpler. However, if every test was brewing their own setup code, all behavior risks to be inconsistent and brittle. For example, I haven't mentioned it, but the prior version also silently conflicted with the effort from https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34439. Also, without the cleanup, it may introduce an out-of-storage issue (if the bench is run in a loop, similar to the symptoms of https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34445)

I think generally it is best to come up with common re-usable concepts and then apply them consistently in the whole codebase.

If you think the docs imply that the common test utils are not applicable to bench tests, the docs can be improved. But the intent is certainly that the common test utils are to be used for all QA efforts.
nit: not sure we really need this helper? It's a one liner called in two places.

This seems like a basic `CCoinsViewCache` test. Is this behavior not covered elsewhere? If not, should we extend this to test that `AccessCoin`, `GetCoin`, `HaveCoin`, `SpendCoin` do not access base when the coin is cached?

comments.body: Done, thanks
I don't like duplication, but I agree it's not needed here

`HaveInputs()` had no unit coverage at all.
These new tests cover the "cache hit should not consult base" and "cache miss should consult `base` via `GetCoin()`" usecases - if you think there's a better location for these, let me know.
I've extended it with the mentioned methods, thanks

From https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320/commits/8c57687f8626a0242d5488a7a95722895ec5fafc commit message:

> `ExistsImpl` was removed since it duplicates `CDBWrapper::ReadImpl` (except that it copies the resulting string on success, but that will be needed for caching anyway).

I was thrown off by the last part of this sentence because it's not clear why caching is being mentioned here. I believe you were referring to the fact that with this PR, the `HaveCoin` will lead to a  `Read` call instead of an `Exists` call and will cache the result so we can ignore the cost of the copy when doing the first `HaveCoin` call.

If I am correct, can you make your justification clearer in the commit message? To state clearly that `Read` is used over `Exists` and the result is cached so the cost of the copy can be ignored.

Thanks for the hint, I simplified this into a single `ccoins_cache_behavior` test using a dummy spy base view. It covers the two behaviors that weren’t explicitly pinned elsewhere: on cache miss, `HaveInputs()` must use base `GetCoin()` (not `HaveCoin()`), and once cached (including prefilled cache), lookups stay in cache without extra base calls, with `SpendCoin()` updating cache state as expected. So this keeps the original intent while adopting your simplification and removing the DB setup/duplication.

https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320/commits/277c57f0c5900fb8bc636ce7632452b245d296c4:

I don't think we need `base.allow_getcoin`, because the `BOOST_CHECK_EQUAL(base.getcoin_calls, 1);` line below would fail if another `base.GetCoin()` call was made.

https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320/commits/c0c94ec9860ac121e342e6c2102ffaf30630c244:

New commit message looks good to me.

[277c57f](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320/changes/277c57f) _test: add `HaveInputs` call-path unit tests_:

Good point, taken - also rebased in the meantime, you can diff with `B=ff338fdb53a66ab40a36e1277e7371941fc89840 A=dd76338a57b9b1169ac27f7b783d6d0d4c6e38ab && git fetch origin $B $A && git range-diff --creation-factor=95 $B...$A`

> [c68262b](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320/changes/c68262b6cf068d55faf555f4dcc51a92d53a4fdb) _coins: delegate `CCoinsViewDB::HaveCoin` to `GetCoin`_:

`CCoinsViewDB` is the lowest 'cache' level, without caching, so here it makes sense to perform an efficient existence-check only in the database. It makes no sense to bring the full value, as here it cannot be saved to a cache. This method may actually never be called, but the argument still holds. It could be checked wether this method is needed at all (the higher-level cache uses `CCoinsViewDB::GetCoin`), and if not, replace it with a throw.

> [a5a9958](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320/changes/a5a9958aae7a6bad82361e967f8b8d7e3a0852f2) _validation: merge `PeekCoin` into `GetCoin`_:

In this PR both `HaveCoin` and `GetCoin` are used, yet here `GetCoin` behavior is forked into 'returning' and 'peek-only' versions. I think the current `HaveCoin` could be used for the (caching) peek-only version, and then there is no need for changing `GetCoin`.
I have removed the `GetCoin`/`PeekCoin` merge, can you pleas check if this is better? Not sure I fully understood what you meant here...

I expand on this in a top-level comment.
What I meant is that I don't see the benefit of this change ('Exists' is still there), but I see a small regression: that the value is returned unnecessarily to one higher level, and the exists-only intent is lost.
Please let me know if this still applies in the latest push.
I think I would still prefer if the `HaveCoin` member function and virtual member function would be removed, and test code just call `GetCoin(...)` directly.

Andrew specifically mentioned he considers it the right level of abstraction, see: https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34320#issuecomment-3815084392
@andrewtoth is that still your preference here?
Yes, I still see no reason for ccc0656660bfae0c571534466ad2a4d0e2211b96 . Especially that `CDBWrapper::Exsist` calls into `!!ReadRaw()`.
It is fine to keep it as a member for the coins view cache imo, but I don't think we should carry around dead virtual functions in the rest of them.
